concepts.headline: Structure beats substance. Every single time.
concepts.subtext: Analysis of 10,000 LinkedIn posts. The clearest structure wins, not the best insight.
concepts.layout_description: Centered statement layout. FLYWHEEL watermark at opacity 0.03 behind content. Headline in 84px weight 600, split across two lines for dramatic effect. Thin warm sand accent bar 2px above the headline, fading right. Subtext in 16px secondary color below. Particle dots scattered in upper-right and lower-right periphery at low opacity. Flywheel logo mark bottom-left at 48px from edges.

layout_spec.headline_position: center, vertically offset slightly above midpoint
layout_spec.accent_placement: 2px horizontal bar above headline, left-anchored, fades to transparent at 40% width
layout_spec.whitespace_distribution: heavy top and bottom breathing room, 30% left, 30% right margins

concepts.color_usage: Background #ffffff. Headline #000000. Subtext #6b7a7c. Accent bar #d7c8af. Watermark #000000 at opacity 0.03. Particle dots #000000 at opacity 0.06.
concepts.reasoning: The post's core argument is a single provocative claim. A bold statement card lets the idea land with full typographic authority, mirroring the post's own lesson: clarity and structure over noise.
visualization_goals: Show the correlation between post structure clarity and engagement rates across 10,000 analyzed LinkedIn posts
Contrast engagement levels between wall-of-text posts versus structured posts with visual breaks
Illustrate the hierarchy of factors driving LinkedIn post performance, with structure ranked above substance/insight quality
Visualize the reader attention drop-off rate for unstructured posts versus retention curve for well-structured posts during fast scrolling behavior

concepts.headline: What actually drives LinkedIn engagement.
concepts.subtext: Structured posts outperform unstructured ones by a wide margin, across 10,000 posts analyzed.
concepts.layout_description: Left-anchored hero layout. Headline and subtext occupy the left 45% of the canvas, top-aligned with generous padding. Horizontal bar chart occupies the right 50%, vertically centered. Bars are square-edged, rendered in black at varying opacity to create tonal hierarchy. The top bar (Clear structure) is fullest black, bottom bar (Wall of text) is lightest gray. DM Mono labels on bar values. Particle network at lower-right corner at opacity 0.08. FLYWHEEL watermark behind chart at opacity 0.03. Warm accent bar 2px at top-left of chart area.

layout_spec.headline_position: top-left at 15% from top, 64px from left edge
layout_spec.stat_position: right half, center-right at 55% from left
layout_spec.accent_placement: 2px bar at top of chart column, left edge of chart block
layout_spec.whitespace_distribution: 55% left narrative, 45% chart, generous vertical padding
layout_spec.emphasis_area: top bar in chart, highest value

concepts.color_usage: Background #ffffff. Headline #000000. Subtext #6b7a7c. Bars #000000 descending to #c8cccd. Bar labels DM Mono #6b7a7c. Accent bar #d7c8af. Watermark at opacity 0.03.
concepts.reasoning: The post is grounded in a 10,000-post analysis. A bar chart makes the structural hierarchy of engagement drivers concrete and scannable, embodying the post's own lesson: clear visual structure makes content readable.
visualization_goals: Show the correlation between post structure clarity and engagement rates across 10,000 analyzed LinkedIn posts
Contrast engagement levels between wall-of-text posts versus structured posts with visual breaks
Illustrate the hierarchy of factors driving LinkedIn post performance, with structure ranked above substance/insight quality
Visualize the reader attention drop-off rate for unstructured posts versus retention curve for well-structured posts during fast scrolling behavior

concepts.headline: Ten thousand posts. One clear winner.
concepts.subtext: Structured posts with visual breaks and a strong hook consistently outperform posts with superior insight but poor formatting.
data_points: Structure clarity is the top predictor of engagement
Wall-of-text posts are invisible in a fast scroll feed
A hook in line one determines if the post gets read
Pattern recognition drives the eye before the brain engages
concepts.layout_description: Stacked list layout. Eyebrow label 'LINKEDIN ANALYSIS' in DM Mono 11px uppercase at top-left. Headline large at 72px below, left-anchored, occupying 60% of canvas width. Subtext in 16px secondary below headline. Four data points below as a sparse left-aligned list, each with a 2px square marker in warm sand. Right side is largely empty with a subtle particle network at opacity 0.07. FLYWHEEL watermark behind at opacity 0.02. Warm gradient footer at bottom 80px.

layout_spec.accent_placement: square 2px markers left of each list item, warm sand color
layout_spec.whitespace_distribution: 60% left content, 40% right open with particles, 20% bottom breathing room

concepts.color_usage: Background #ffffff. Eyebrow #6b7a7c. Headline #000000. Subtext #6b7a7c. List markers #d7c8af. List text #000000. Particle dots #000000 at opacity 0.07. Watermark #000000 at opacity 0.02.
concepts.reasoning: The post enumerates several structural elements that drive engagement, making a headline-plus-list treatment natural. The sparse layout demonstrates the very principle being argued: structured, readable, and visually navigable.
visualization_goals: Show the correlation between post structure clarity and engagement rates across 10,000 analyzed LinkedIn posts
Contrast engagement levels between wall-of-text posts versus structured posts with visual breaks
Illustrate the hierarchy of factors driving LinkedIn post performance, with structure ranked above substance/insight quality
Visualize the reader attention drop-off rate for unstructured posts versus retention curve for well-structured posts during fast scrolling behavior

selection_reasoning: The bar chart concept is the strongest because it transforms the post's central claim into immediate visual evidence. The descending bar values make the hierarchy of engagement drivers self-evident at a glance, enacting the post's own lesson that structure and clear visual pattern drive comprehension. The left-anchored editorial layout, DM Mono data labels, and restrained monochrome palette all align precisely with Flywheel's Swiss-design identity.
